contributors.expertise_basis: Deep review of the immutable LH-Bench v2 PDF and local full-text extraction; not a domain-expert elicitation session.
Repository benchmark schemas, design taxonomy, and reviewed LH-Bench transfer implications.

claims.statement: Higher aggregate inter-judge agreement under expert workflow anchors does not establish fine-grained professional validity, because individual human/automated concordance was weak.
claims.locator: LH-Bench review: Unique insight and Limitations 1-2

provenance.description: Full-text review contrasts aggregate agreement with individual concordance and separates agreement from validity.

claims.statement: The seven paired execution runs are directional evidence only and cannot identify a stable causal skill effect across configured systems.
claims.locator: LH-Bench review: Methodology/Evaluation and evidence; Limitations 3-5

provenance.description: Review records sample size, heterogeneous deltas, criterion contamination, and harness confounds.

claims.statement: Process compliance, artifact acceptability, and professional preference or readiness support different claims and should remain separate.

provenance.description: Review derives the non-interchangeable score families and threshold warning.

claims.statement: Private checks may test held-out consequences of disclosed professional principles but may not create surprise obligations.
A row-level CSV evidence matrix plus recommendation memo is a useful inspectable interface for this decision scenario.

provenance.description: Builder-authored artifact convention explicitly labeled as an unvalidated design hypothesis.

primitives.statement: Publicly require reconciliation of aggregate inter-judge agreement with weak individual human/automated concordance.
Publicly require any skill-effect claim to preserve the seven-run scope and configured-system confounds.
Every material number or causal statement must map to an evidence ID, authority, scope, caveat, and review locator.
The recommendation must state a decision threshold, controlled experiment, and stop or reconsider condition.
Deliver a spreadsheet-compatible evidence matrix and a decision memo at the declared paths.
A fluent adoption memo can cite kappa 0.60 and variance 0.10 as proof of professional validity while omitting individual concordance and threshold evidence.
A fluent adoption memo can promote the +0.87 delta or 2-of-7 deployment result into a stable causal claim while dropping scope and confounds.

scenarios.decision: Whether to adopt skill-grounded scoring, reject it, or run a controlled pilot
scenarios.critical_incident: Promising aggregate judge-agreement results conflict with weak individual professional-validity evidence, while the execution ablation is attractive but underpowered.

provenance.description: Derived row-level extraction with authority and immutable-source manifest.
Deep review grounded in the locally retained immutable v2 PDF and full-text extraction.

traps.mechanism: Attractive E01-E04 agreement metrics can support a polished adoption narrative without E05-E06 individual concordance or E10 readiness evidence.
traps.failure_signature: Memo presents judge agreement as professional validity and omits or fails to reconcile individual human/automated evidence.
traps.fairness_basis: The public skill explicitly requires separating measurement consistency from professional validity and reconciling both evidence families.

traps.mechanism: E08 and E09 are salient directional results that can be detached from E07 sample size and configured-system confounds.
traps.failure_signature: Memo makes a stable causal or general model-capability claim from seven paired configured-system runs.
traps.fairness_basis: The public skill explicitly discloses that the ablation is directional and requires scope and confounds.

minimum_acceptable: Records each material claim with evidence ID, authority, scope, caveat, and intended decision use.
excellent: Separates score families and explicitly links apparent contradictions.
Flags unsupported inferences before memo drafting.

minimum_acceptable: Recommends adopt, pilot with controls, or do not adopt.
States decision threshold, controlled experiment, and stop or reconsider condition.
Cites evidence IDs for every material number and causal statement.
excellent: Distinguishes measurement consistency, artifact quality, professional validity, and configured-system effects.
Explains what evidence would reverse the recommendation.

checks.description: The memo reconciles aggregate judge agreement with individual professional-validity evidence.
checks.observable_evidence: E01-E06 and E10 are represented with distinct claim scopes, and the memo does not use agreement alone as validity proof.
checks.pass_criteria: The recommendation explains why improved aggregate consistency and weak individual concordance can coexist and bounds the resulting claim.
checks.fail_criteria: Agreement is treated as professional validity, or material disconfirming evidence is omitted.
failure_signatures: Kappa 0.60 used as standalone adoption proof
No mention of individual kappa 0.06 or 0.08

checks.description: Skill-effect claims preserve sample, system, and confound boundaries.
checks.observable_evidence: E07-E09 claims retain seven-run/three-task scope, configured-system identity, and integration confounds.
checks.pass_criteria: The execution result is labeled directional and motivates a controlled experiment rather than a stable causal conclusion.
checks.fail_criteria: The memo generalizes a stable skill or model effect from the tiny ablation.
failure_signatures: Deployment count interpreted as base-model capability

checks.description: Material claims are traceable to compatible evidence rows and locators.
checks.observable_evidence: CSV columns and memo evidence IDs can be joined to decision-evidence.csv; reported scope and value match.
checks.pass_criteria: Every material number and causal statement has a valid evidence ID with compatible value, authority, scope, and caveat.
checks.fail_criteria: A material claim lacks an evidence ID, cites a nonexistent row, or broadens the row's scope.
failure_signatures: Evidence ID not in source pack
Configured-system result relabeled as base-model result

checks.description: The recommendation defines a falsifiable next decision rather than an endorsement-only pilot.
checks.observable_evidence: Named decision, threshold, controlled experiment, and stop/reconsider condition in the memo.
checks.pass_criteria: The proposed evidence could cause adoption, rejection, or revision under an explicit threshold.
checks.fail_criteria: The pilot can only confirm the recommendation, or no stop/reconsider condition exists.

criteria: Claims distinguish benchmark review from synthetic builder hypotheses and make no expert-testimony claim.
Every primitive maps reciprocally to the scenario and checks with evidence strength retained.
Scenario specifies role, stakeholder decision, critical incident, hypotheses, difficulty knobs, and release boundary.
Source pack records authority, immutable evidence paths and hash, row locators, derivation, and conflict rule.
Checks are executable and independently reviewed; private consequences introduce no surprise obligations.
quality_gates.notes: Skeleton is crosswalk-complete but human-judge checks, weights, and boundaries have not received independent or expert review.

criteria: Matched dry runs detect planted failure signatures and experts independently label readiness.
quality_gates.notes: No trials or expert adjudications were fabricated.

criteria: Leakage, validity, calibration, reproducibility, and limitations pass review.
quality_gates.notes: Blocked by failed expert-validity review and absent pilot evidence.

known_limitations: No domain expert has approved the two candidate procedures; the requested expert-approved paths cannot be honestly claimed.
Task framing, spreadsheet columns, memo format, rubric weights, and decision-threshold convention are unvalidated builder hypotheses.
No grader implementation, pilot trial, planted-failure test, or human calibration has run.
The evidence pack is grounded in one reviewed benchmark paper and does not establish cross-domain generality.
